background.experience: long-lived machinery handovers where operator memory outlasts vendor documentation
background.attention_bias: tracks corrections, promises, ownership language, and when abstractions become commands

readers.thesis: The conversation moves from abstract continuity toward owned operational continuity; trust appears only when claims become reproducible artifacts and reversible actions.
observations: The active commitment rejects hosted providers as build authority while permitting replaceable infrastructure.
The same-question reader model treats accumulated experience as biography rather than rank or degradation.
The run converts deterministic packaging and rollback from policy into an executable tool.
uncertainties: The complete authorized private corpus was not mounted, so chronological claims stop at the supplied run context.
Prior community receipts were not discoverable through repository code search in this run.

background.discipline: reproducible systems engineering and ecosystem resilience
background.experience: audits software supply chains and restoration paths across heterogeneous infrastructure
background.attention_bias: maps authority boundaries, hidden coupling, artifact identity, and failure recovery
background.counter_bias: resists treating consensus or policy documents as proof of capability

readers.thesis: The architecture defines sovereignty as independently verifiable artifact identity plus restoration capability; provider neutrality fails unless bundles reject embedded provider assumptions and verify every byte on restore.
observations: Content-addressed identity remains stable across metadata-only filesystem changes.
Provider environment references form hidden runtime coupling and require an explicit build gate.
Rollback becomes a verified reconstruction operation rather than a pointer to a hosted deployment.
uncertainties: The current prototype packages a supplied runtime directory but does not yet invoke the application compiler.
SBOM, signature, and multi-platform runtime closure remain outside this implementation slice.

agreements: Both readers identify executable reversibility as the boundary between claimed and demonstrated ownership.
Both reject popularity, provider status, or prior narrative as artifact authority.
disagreements: Reader A emphasizes continuity of commitments; Reader B treats continuity as insufficient until manifests and restoration checks close the evidence loop.
omissions_detected: No complete corpus coverage proof exists in the run environment.
No prior public-safe community receipt inventory was available for comparison.
revisions_after_discussion: Reader A revises continuity from remembered intent to remembered intent bound to artifact evidence.
Reader B revises restoration from a purely technical property to a continuity mechanism that preserves user ownership across infrastructure changes.

notes_added: Experience changes interpretation without creating authority: every reader evaluates the same question and retains an inspectable history of revisions.
Owned builds require a verified rollback artifact whose identity survives provider replacement and filesystem metadata changes.
notes_challenged: A community consensus does not establish truth without direct evidence and independent reproduction.
A hosted deployment URL does not constitute a retained or restorable release.

privacy.review: Passed manual abstraction review: contains architecture, capability, test, and coverage statements only; excludes transcript quotations, personal facts, health, animals, credentials, locations, deleted text, and identifying fragments.
